name: Latiolais v. Huntington Ingalls, Inc. (Latiolais II)
summary: Held that failure-to-warn claims against a military contractor were 'related to' the contractor's federally directed act of installing asbestos on Navy ships, even though the federal government did not specifically direct the warnings.
significance: Fifth Circuit en banc decision cited as the prototypical example of how the 2011 'relating to' amendment expanded removal in asbestos failure-to-warn cases against military contractors.
